strengths: Clearly identifies three specific objects and provides recognisable real-world contexts for each (chopsticks, Burj Khalifa, handwritten to-do list).

Basic links are made between each object and the chosen IA prompt through personal experience, supported by illustrative examples (e.g. chopstick lengths, multicultural classroom insights, procrastination narrative).
Consistent structure and clear headings for each object enhance readability and orientation.
Use of original photographs engages the reader and grounds the discussion in tangible real-world artefacts.
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely narrative and descriptive, lacking deployment of TOK terminology (e.g. ways of knowing, experiential vs. propositional knowledge) and theoretical depth.
Justifications for inclusion are repetitive in argument (experience shaping knowledge) and do not interrelate the objects or build a broader perspective.
Real-world contexts are sometimes vague (missing dates, settings, confusing fraction notation) and assertions (e.g. digital lists vs. paper lists) lack external evidence or scholarly support.
No concluding synthesis to draw the three objects together or explicitly restate the prompt at the end of each objectâs discussion.
Critical questioning of knowledge claims is absent; the exhibition does not consider limitations or alternative perspectives on how experience informs knowledge.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates identification of three relevant objects with some explanation of links to the IA prompt and basic justification supported by concrete examples, aligning with the 3â4 band descriptor. However, it lacks precision in TOK analysis, theoretical terminology, critical depth and synthesis across objects. These characteristics warrant a top-of-band score of 4, reflecting a satisfactory but uneven exhibition that meets some criteria for coherence and evidence yet falls short of consistent clarity, critical evaluation and conceptual rigor.

annotations.comment: The prompt is stated clearly, but the student could explicitly refer back to it within each objectâs discussion to strengthen alignment with the IA question.
The photograph of the chopsticks effectively illustrates the object, but including a caption with date or location would ground its real-world context more precisely.
The object title uses singular âchopstickâ but the discussion refers to multiple chopsticks. Revise to âchopsticksâ for consistency and clarity.
The inclusion of the fraction $$\frac{36}{100}$$ to denote different chopstick lengths is confusing without explanation. Clarify its relevance or remove it.
The discussion describes cultural rules well, but it remains descriptive. Incorporate TOK terminology (e.g., âexperiential knowledgeâ vs. âpropositional knowledgeâ) to deepen analysis.
Linking the legend of new-year chopsticks to collective tradition is a strong cultural insight, demonstrating how individual practice contributes to shared knowledge.
The analysis repeatedly emphasizes experience shaping knowledge but lacks new angles in the second paragraph. Vary reasons or introduce a contrasting perspective.
The connection to the prompt is implicit but not explicitly restated here. Reaffirm how this object addresses âpersonal experience and knowledgeâ to reinforce focus.
The title clearly introduces Object 2 and situates the focus on Burj Khalifa, aiding reader navigation through the exhibition.
The image of the Burj Khalifa is impactful, but a caption indicating time and context (e.g., date taken, viewpoint) would enhance the real-world grounding.
The section header clearly labels the exhibition, providing immediate orientation for the reader. This helps frame the overall presentation of the three objects.
Linking expatriate life to multicultural perspective shows how immersion generates tacit understanding beyond textbooks.
The claim that experiential learning is âworth a lot more than just readingâ is asserted but not evidenced. Provide specific examples or scholarly support.
Consider integrating TOK concepts such as âperspectiveâ or âculture as a lensâ to deepen the theoretical grounding of this analysis.
The second paragraph effectively expands on religious diversity shaping knowledge, showing a deeper dimension of intercultural understanding.
The ideas about culture and religion overlap substantially with the first paragraph. Introduce a contrasting perspective or a limitation to avoid redundancy.
Neither paragraph explicitly restates the prompt. After each section, reconnect examples back to âpersonal experience and knowledgeâ for clarity.
The heading for Object 3 clearly signals a new focus and maintains consistency in structure across all objects.
The photo of the to-do list conveys the object, but the visualâs real-world context (e.g., date, setting) remains vagueâadding detail would reinforce specificity.
The narrative of procrastination and the to-do list is engaging and shows clear causal linkage between experience and practical knowledge.
To deepen TOK analysis, link the to-do list example to specific ways of knowing (e.g., memory, reasoning) and discuss their roles in organizing knowledge.
The assertion that paper lists work but digital ones fail is anecdotal. Strengthen with evidence or a brief discussion of cognitive research on note-taking.
The mention of group-work applicability is promising. Expand this idea to show how collaborative contexts might challenge or extend individual knowledge.
Each individual object is justified, but the exhibition would benefit from a concluding statement that synthesizes how these objects collectively illustrate the prompt.
Throughout the exhibition, the analysis remains narrative. Introduce critical questioning of whether personal experience alone suffices to justify reliable knowledge.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
